name: perceived_indigenous_discrimination
description: Measures the degree to which respondents perceive that indigenous people face discrimination, unequal treatment, and denial of rights in Mexican society, capturing both attitudinal recognition of discrimination and assessments of systemic inequality.

name: social_distance_toward_indigenous_people
description: Measures the willingness of non-indigenous respondents to engage in close personal, domestic, or professional relationships with indigenous people, reflecting affective prejudice and behavioral exclusion tendencies.

name: indigenous_cultural_integration_vs_autonomy
description: Captures respondents' preferences regarding whether indigenous peoples should assimilate into mainstream Mexican society or maintain distinct cultural, political, and linguistic autonomy, including views on self-governance, land rights, and language of education.

name: stereotyping_and_social_image_of_indigenous_people
description: Measures the content and valence of respondents' mental representations of indigenous people, including occupational stereotypes, family-level attitudes, and perceived advantages and disadvantages of indigenous identity.

name: national_economic_outlook
description: Measures respondents' retrospective and prospective evaluations of Mexico's overall economic situation, capturing both sociotropic economic assessments and economic expectations.
